missions.title: Arcana Awakening
missions.description: Complete all onboarding modules to fully master Arcana. Each module unlocks a new layer of the system — from status tracking to AI integration and remote access.

missions.title: Status Calibration
missions.description: Set up your user profile and configure the status panel. Start by editing user_profile.json with your username and birth date, then define at least one metric in status_metric_definitions.json and record its value.

missions.title: Mission Control Online
missions.description: Activate the mission system. Accept your first mission by setting its status to 'active', then configure a main menu widget so your most urgent objective is always visible on the home screen.

missions.title: Persona Awakening
missions.description: Explore the achievement and skill tree system. Load a content pack that matches your interests, then unlock your first achievement. The skill tree grows as you accumulate achievements.

missions.title: Gallery Online
missions.description:
Set up the Gallery module. The Gallery aggregates media history from JSON source files — each source is a file produced by an import script or written manually.

Quickstart with import scripts:
  1. cp scripts/config.example.json scripts/config.json
  2. Fill in your IDs: bangumi_uid (Bangumi profile URL), steam_api_key + steam_id (steamcommunity.com/dev/apikey), douban_id (Douban profile URL)
  3. Run one or more scripts:
       python scripts/fetch_bangumi.py
       python scripts/fetch_steam.py --detailed
       python scripts/fetch_douban.py --status all
  4. Register the output in gallery_sources.json:
       { "id": "bangumi", "name": "Anime", "path": "gallery/bangumi_anime.json", "media_type": "anime", "icon": "🎌" }

Objective: complete the arcana::setup_gallery_source achievement (register at least one source).

missions.title: Inventory Log
missions.description:
Connect an item directory to the Items module. The Items module was built around the author's Obsidian vault — it reads plain .md files with YAML frontmatter, so Obsidian users can point it at an existing note directory and get started immediately. If you don't use Obsidian, just create a folder of .md files in any text editor; the format is plain Markdown.

Each Markdown file in a registered directory becomes a tracked item — Arcana reads the YAML frontmatter and computes derived fields automatically.

Setup:
  1. Edit item_sources.json and add a source entry:
       { "id": "electronics", "name": "Electronics", "path": "/absolute/path/to/Obsidian/Items/Electronics" }
  2. Each .md file in that directory is one item. Frontmatter fields: 名称, 品牌, 价格, 购入日期, 购入方式, 类别, 颜色. Any other fields are stored as extras.
  3. Example item file (MacBook Pro.md):
       ---
       品牌: Apple
       价格: 14999
       购入日期: 2023-11-01
       购入方式: Apple 官网
       类别: 电子产品
       ---

Arcana auto-computes: days_owned (today − purchase date) and daily_cost (price ÷ days owned).

Objective: complete the arcana::setup_item_source achievement (register at least one source).

missions.title: Velvet Room Access
missions.description:
Configure the AI provider so the agent can read and update your data. This is the most important setup step — once working, the AI guides all further progress.

Setup:
  1. Create ~/.arcana/agent_config.json:
       {
         "api_key": "your-key",
         "base_url": "https://api.deepseek.com",
         "model": "deepseek-chat"
       }
  2. Recommended provider: DeepSeek V4 (cost-effective, Anthropic-API-compatible). For Anthropic: base_url https://api.anthropic.com, model claude-sonnet-4-5-20251001. You can also set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY as an environment variable.
  3. Run /velvet-room in any AI coding agent that supports slash commands (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, or even openclaw (untested)) to verify: tell it what you've set up so far and let it update your achievements.

Objective: complete arcana::setup_ai_provider, then arcana::first_velvet_room.

missions.title: Phantom Thieves HQ
missions.description: Master the AI skill system. Use /velvet-room to report progress, /phan-site to generate mission proposals, and /pack-manager to create or customize an achievement pack. These three skills form the core AI workflow.

missions.title: Navigator Online
missions.description:
Deploy the Telegram bot so you can update Arcana from anywhere via your phone.

Step-by-step:
  1. Create a bot: open Telegram → search @BotFather → /newbot → follow prompts → copy the token
  2. Get your Telegram user ID: message @userinfobot on Telegram
  3. Add to ~/.arcana/agent_config.json:
       {
         "api_key": "...",
         "telegram": {
           "token": "123456789:ABCdef...",
           "allow_from": ["YOUR_NUMERIC_ID"]
         }
       }
  4. Build the bot:
       cargo build --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --bin agent-telegram --release
  5. Run it (keep it running on your desktop):
       ./src-tauri/target/release/agent-telegram
  6. Message the bot on Telegram — describe something to update, e.g. '刚完成了 Navigator Online 的配置'

The bot uses long-polling — no server or webhook required. Messages go: Telegram → bot → agent loop → arcana-data CLI → local JSON files.

Objective: complete arcana::setup_telegram_bot, then arcana::remote_update_via_im.
